title: Short-run aggregate supply
description: Practice what you've learned about sticky wages, menu costs, and short-run aggregate supply shocks in this exercise.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following best describes something that would cause an increase in output along a short-run aggregate supply (SRAS) curve?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: An increase in the consumer price index (CPI)
An increase in the price of a single good
A decrease in the wage rate
Firms spend less on capital equipment
An increase in business taxes

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following best describes the relationship illustrated by the short-run aggregate supply (SRAS) curve?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The positive relationship between the price level and the quantity of output produced
The positive relationship between the unemployment rate and the amount of output supplied
The negative relationship between the price level and the quantity of output produced
The positive relationship between the price of a single good and the quantity supplied by producers
The fixed amount of all output that will be produced, which does not respond to the price level

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following best describes why the short-run aggregate supply (SRAS) curve is upward sloping?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Prices and nominal wages are slow to adjust to changes in the price level
Nominal wages adjust quickly to changes in the price level
Business spending on new capital will increase when the price level decreases
The real value of assets increases as the price level decreases
Firms respond to inflation by producing less

assessment_items.question_content:
**How will nominal wages respond to a decrease in the price level in the short run?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Nominal wages will decrease by the same change as the decrease in the price level
Nominal wages will decrease by a greater change than the change in the price level
Nominal wages will not change, or will change in an amount smaller than the change in the price level
Nominal wages will increase in an amount equal to the decrease in the price level
Nominal wages will increase in an amount greater than the decrease in the price level

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the scenarios below is a consequence of sticky wages?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The amount of aggregate output supplied increases in response to inflation.
The amount of aggregate output supplied is the same regardless of inflation.
The amount of aggregate output demanded decreases in response to inflation.
The aggregate demand curve is upward sloping.
The short-run aggregate supply curve is downward sloping.

assessment_items.question_content:
[[☃ image 1]]

**Which of the following could cause the change in short run aggregate supply (SRAS) shown in the graph here?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

options.alt: A graph with a vertical axis labeled as Price Level and a horizontal axis labeled as real Gross Domestic Product. There are two upward-sloping curves representing Short-Run Aggregate Supply, labeled as Short-Run Aggregate Supply one and Short-Run Aggregate Supply two, with the second curve positioned above the first and both curves increasing in height as they extend to the right. A horizontal arrow points from Short-Run Aggregate Supply one to Short-Run Aggregate Supply two.

choices.content: The expectation of lower inflation in the future
A decrease in productivity
An increase in the bargaining power of labor unions
An increase in energy prices
An increase in disposable incomes

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following would cause short-run aggregate supply to decrease?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: An increase in the cost of labor
A decrease in business taxes
A decrease in the price of capital
Firms anticipate lower inflation in the future
An increase in consumption

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following types of events shifts the short-run aggregate supply (SRAS) curve to the left?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Any event that makes it more expensive or more difficult to produce goods and services
Any event that makes it less expensive or easier to produce goods and services
Any event that causes consumers to purchase fewer goods and services that is not due to changes in the price level
Any event that causes exports to decrease that is not due to changes in the price level
Any event that causes imports to increase that is not due to changes in the price level

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following types of events shifts the short-run aggregate supply (SRAS) curve to the right?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Any event that makes it less expensive or easier to produce goods and services
Any event that makes it more expensive or more difficult to produce goods and services
Any event that causes consumers to purchase fewer goods and services that is not due to changes in the price level
Any event that causes exports to decrease that is not due to changes in the price level
An increase in the price level in the short run.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following best describes a relationship that is illustrated in the short-run aggregate supply (SRAS) curve?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: There is a short-run tradeoff between inflation and unemployment
There is a long-run relationship between inflation and unemployment
In order to produce more of one good, you must produce less of another good
As the price level increases, the amount of expenditures in a country decreases
In the long run there is no relationship between output and the price level

assessment_items.question_content:
**What happens to inflation and the unemployment rate as you move to the right along the short-run aggregate supply (SRAS) curve?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Inflation increases and the unemployment rate decreases.
Inflation decreases and the unemployment rate increases.
Inflation decreases and the unemployment rate doesn’t change.
Inflation increases and unemployment doesn’t change.
Inflation doesn’t change and unemployment doesn’t change.

assessment_items.question_content:
**What happens to inflation and unemployment as you move to the left along the short-run aggregate supply (SRAS) curve?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Inflation decreases and unemployment increases.
Inflation increases and unemployment decreases.
Inflation increases and unemployment doesn’t change.
Inflation decreases and unemployment doesn’t change.
Inflation doesn’t change and unemployment doesn’t change.

assessment_items.question_content:
[[☃ image 1]]

The figure here shows two points along a country's SRAS curve.

**Which of the following best describes a tradeoff that a country will experience when it moves from point E to point F?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

options.alt: A graph with a vertical axis labeled "P L" and a horizontal axis labeled "real GDP"; a straight line representing "S R A S" slopes upward from left to right; two points, labeled "E" and "F," are marked on the line with dashed horizontal lines extending to the vertical axis, indicating their respective values for "P L" and "real GDP."

choices.content: A decrease in the unemployment rate, but an increase in inflation
An increase in the unemployment rate, but a decrease in inflation
A decrease in unemployment, but a decrease in inflation
An increase in unemployment, but an increase in inflation
An increase in economic growth, but an increase in  inflation

assessment_items.question_content:
**What happens to the rate of inflation and unemployment as a country moves from point Y to point X in this graph?**

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/c9017208d112131d8a87d13dd5b6df87eded1464)

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The rate of inflation decreases and unemployment increases.
The rate of inflation increases and unemployment decreases.
The rate of inflation decreases and the unemployment rate doesn’t change.
The rate of inflation increases and the unemployment rate doesn’t change.
The rate of inflation doesn’t change and the unemployment rate doesn’t change.

assessment_items.question_content:
**What happens to inflation and unemployment if a country moves from point A to point B on the short-run aggregate supply (SRAS) curve shown here?**

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/8836638771ad0e1f45022d2419a8e9b49895b4fe)

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The rate of inflation increases and unemployment decreases.
The rate of inflation decreases and unemployment increases.
The rate of inflation increases and the unemployment rate doesn’t change.
The rate of inflation decreases, and the unemployment rate doesn’t change.
The rate of inflation doesn’t change, and the unemployment rate doesn’t change.
